response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior generalist with strong TypeScript/Node.js and DLang backgrounds, but the role requires deep JVM expertise plus hands-on experience with Java, Kotlin, and the Spring ecosystem. The resume lists Java only as a general skill with no substantial Spring, Kotlin, JVM-internals, or IDE-platform work. The lone IDE-adjacent experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI in 2014-2015) does not translate to modern JVM tooling. There is a large gap between the candidate's strengths and the position's core technical requirements.
strengths: Long senior-level career across complex full-stack and systems projects
Prior exposure to building an IDE (LabWindows/CVI) and developer tooling/MCP work
Strong craftsmanship signals: TDD, CI/CD, workshops, open-source maintainership
concerns: No demonstrated Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience
No Kotlin and only superficial Java usage across the career
No evidence of JVM internals, language tooling, or IDE platform (IntelliJ/Eclipse) work
